17	func TestAgentExecuteLoopUsesProjectRootForNoWorkScan(t *testing.T) {
18		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
19		subdir := filepath.Join(env.Dir, "nested", "path")
20		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(subdir, 0o755))
21	
22		factory := NewCommandFactory(subdir)
23		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
24	
25		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-loop", "--local", "--json")
26		require.NoError(t, err)
27	
28		var res struct {
29			ProjectRoot string `json:"project_root"`
30			NoReadyWork bool   `json:"no_ready_work"`
31			Attempts    int    `json:"attempts"`
32		}
33		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
34		assert.Equal(t, env.Dir, res.ProjectRoot)
35		assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork)
36		assert.Equal(t, 0, res.Attempts)
37	}
38	
39	func TestInvokeExecuteBeadFromLoopParsesJSONAmidWarnings(t *testing.T) {
40		workDir := t.TempDir()
41		// Init git repo so HEAD can be resolved
42		out, err := exec.Command("git", "init", workDir).CombinedOutput()
43		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
44		// Create an initial commit so HEAD exists
45		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "README.md"), []byte("# test"), 0o644))
46		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", "-A").CombinedOutput()
47		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
48		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "init").CombinedOutput()
49		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
50	
51		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
52			ID:     "my-bead",
53			Title:  "Test bead",
54			Status: bead.StatusOpen,
55		})
56		// Commit the beads.jsonl so it's in the worktree snapshot
57		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl").CombinedOutput()
58		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
59		out, err = exec.Command("git", "-C", workDir, "-c", "user.name=Test", "-c", "user.email=test@test.com", "commit", "-m", "add beads").CombinedOutput()
60		require.NoError(t, err, string(out))
61	
62		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
63			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
64			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111",
65			dirty:       true,
66		}
67		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
68	
69		res, err := agent.ExecuteBead(context.Background(), workDir, "my-bead", agent.ExecuteBeadOptions{AgentRunner: runner}, git)
70		require.NoError(t, err)
71		assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
72		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
73	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8		"testing"
9	
10		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
11		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
12		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
13		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
14	)
15	
16	// TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier verifies the full
17	// execute-bead-loop → review-outcomes pipeline: when the executor returns
18	// success and the reviewer APPROVEs, the bead carries both a kind:routing
19	// event (with resolved_provider/resolved_model) and a kind:review event (with
20	// APPROVE in summary), and computeReviewOutcomes attributes that review to
21	// the executor's harness/model tier with approvals=1.
22	func TestReviewEvidenceApproveAttributesToTier(t *testing.T) {
23		dir := t.TempDir()
24		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
25		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
26	
27		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
28		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
29		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-rev-approve", Title: "approve target"}))
30	
31		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
32			Store: store,
33			Executor: agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
34				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
35					BeadID:    beadID,
36					Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
37					SessionID: "sess-approve",
38					ResultRev: "aabbccdd",
39					Harness:   "claude",
40					Provider:  "claude",
41					Model:     "sonnet",
42				}, nil
43			}),
44			Reviewer: agent.BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*agent.ReviewResult, error) {
45				return &agent.ReviewResult{Verdict: agent.VerdictApprove, RawOutput: "### Verdict: APPROVE"}, nil
46			}),
47		}
48	
49		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), agent.ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})
50		require.NoError(t, err)
51	
52		events, err := store.Events("ddx-rev-approve")
53		require.NoError(t, err)
54	
55		routingIdx, reviewIdx := -1, -1
56		for i, e := range events {
57			switch e.Kind {
58			case "routing":
59				if routingIdx == -1 {
60					routingIdx = i
61				}
62				var body map[string]any
63				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(e.Body), &body))
64				assert.Equal(t, "claude", body["resolved_provider"])
65				assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", body["resolved_model"])
66			case "review":
67				reviewIdx = i
68				assert.Equal(t, "APPROVE", e.Summary)
69			}
70		}
71		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, routingIdx, 0, "loop must append a kind:routing event before review")
72		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, reviewIdx, 0, "loop must append a kind:review event after APPROVE")
73		assert.Less(t, routingIdx, reviewIdx, "routing must precede review for correct tier attribution")
74	
75		rows, err := computeReviewOutcomes(dir)
76		require.NoError(t, err)
77		require.Len(t, rows, 1)
78		assert.Equal(t, "claude/sonnet", rows[0].Tier)
79		assert.Equal(t, "claude", rows[0].Harness)
80		assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", rows[0].Model)
81		assert.Equal(t, 1, rows[0].Reviews)
82		assert.Equal(t, 1, rows[0].Approvals)
83		assert.Equal(t, 0, rows[0].Rejections)
84		assert.InDelta(t, 1.0, rows[0].ApprovalRate, 0.0001)
85	}
86	
87	// TestReviewEvidenceRequestChangesCountedAsRejection verifies that a
88	// REQUEST_CHANGES verdict is attributed to the same tier as the executor's
89	// report and counted as a rejection in review-outcomes.
90	func TestReviewEvidenceRequestChangesCountedAsRejection(t *testing.T) {
91		dir := t.TempDir()
92		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
93		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
94	
95		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
96		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
97		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-rev-reject", Title: "reject target"}))
98	
99		worker := &agent.ExecuteBeadWorker{
100			Store: store,
101			Executor: agent.ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(_ context.Context, beadID string) (agent.ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
102				return agent.ExecuteBeadReport{
103					BeadID:    beadID,
104					Status:    agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
105					SessionID: "sess-reject",
106					ResultRev: "11223344",
107					Harness:   "claude",
108					Provider:  "claude",
109					Model:     "sonnet",
110				}, nil
111			}),
112			Reviewer: agent.BeadReviewerFunc(func(_ context.Context, _, _, _, _ string) (*agent.ReviewResult, error) {
113				return &agent.ReviewResult{Verdict: agent.VerdictRequestChanges, RawOutput: "### Verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES\n\n- needs tests"}, nil
114			}),
115		}
116	
117		_, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), agent.ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{Assignee: "worker", Once: true})
118		require.NoError(t, err)
119	
120		events, err := store.Events("ddx-rev-reject")
121		require.NoError(t, err)
122		var hasRouting, hasReview bool
123		for _, e := range events {
124			if e.Kind == "routing" {
125				hasRouting = true
126				var body map[string]any
127				require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(e.Body), &body))
128				assert.Equal(t, "claude", body["resolved_provider"])
129				assert.Equal(t, "sonnet", body["resolved_model"])
130			}
131			if e.Kind == "review" {
132				hasReview = true
133				assert.Equal(t, "REQUEST_CHANGES", e.Summary)
134			}
135		}
136		assert.True(t, hasRouting, "loop must append kind:routing on the reopened-after-review path")
137		assert.True(t, hasReview, "loop must append kind:review with REQUEST_CHANGES summary")
138	
139		rows, err := computeReviewOutcomes(dir)
140		require.NoError(t, err)
141		require.Len(t, rows, 1)
142		assert.Equal(t, "claude/sonnet", rows[0].Tier)
143		assert.Equal(t, 1, rows[0].Reviews)
144		assert.Equal(t, 0, rows[0].Approvals)
145		assert.Equal(t, 1, rows[0].Rejections)
146		assert.InDelta(t, 0.0, rows[0].ApprovalRate, 0.0001)
147	}
